plays.story: Bonzo Lend loses $9M to an oracle-manipulation exploit on Hedera, reigniting debate over DeFi price-feed security

angles.itsalreadypriced: '$9M Gone in Seconds: How a Single Oracle Flaw Drained Bonzo Lend on Hedera' — break down the exploit mechanics for traders, what happens to depositors' funds, and why Hedera's 'enterprise-grade' pitch just took a credibility hit.
angles.itsalreadywhen: 'Anatomy of an Oracle Heist: The 6 Lines of Code That Cost Bonzo Lend $9M' — a technical postmortem for security teams on price-feed manipulation as an attack class, with a checklist for auditing oracle dependencies.

plays.story: A compromised jscrambler 8.14.0 npm release silently drops a Rust-based infostealer during install, hitting the JS supply chain

angles.itsalreadywhen: 'Your Build Pipeline Just Got Owned: Inside the jscrambler npm Supply-Chain Infostealer' — actionable purge/rollback steps for dev teams, plus why obfuscation tools are becoming prime supply-chain targets.
angles.itsalreadypriced: 'If You Run a Web3 Frontend, Check Your node_modules Right Now' — spin the npm compromise toward crypto/dapp devs who lean on jscrambler-style obfuscation, tying it to wallet-drainer and seed-phrase-theft risk.

plays.story: Sly Flourish's 'Prepping and Running Lazy Campaigns' distills a low-effort, high-improv GM prep method

angles.bizzal-games: 'Steal This Prep Sheet: How to Run a Full Session on 20 Minutes of Notes' — a practical breakdown/checklist adapting Sly Flourish's Lazy DM method for time-strapped GMs, with a downloadable template hook.
angles.itsalreadywritten: 'Why Over-Plotting Kills Your Campaign (And What to Write Instead)' — reframe the lazy-prep philosophy as a writing-craft lesson: strong scene skeletons over airtight plots, with examples of what to actually script vs. improvise.

plays.story: EN World's 'Send in the Clowns' column tackles horror-comedy villains and how they land at the table

angles.bizzal-games: 'Running a Clown Villain That Actually Scares Your Players' — a GM's guide to pacing the horror/comedy tonal whiplash at the table, with sample stat blocks and read-aloud cues.
angles.itsalreadywritten: 'The Uncanny Clown: Writing Villains That Are Funny Until They Aren't' — a character-design breakdown of why unsettling-clown villains work narratively, with tips for foreshadowing the turn from silly to sinister.
